feedback_strengths: Strong comparative structure that addresses all requested dimensions (protocol differences, browser support, scalability, and decision criteria) with clear sectioning and practical guidance.
High practical value from concrete code excerpts and workflow patterns (handshake for WebSocket, EventSource usage, SSE message formats) that ground the prose in real implementation details.
Excellent use-case guidance with explicit 'when to choose' and 'when not to use' lists, including hybrid architecture examples that reflect real-world design patterns.
Good breadth across performance, infrastructure, mobile behavior, security, and examples from major operators, making the report useful for architecture decisions.
Clear writing style with a coherent trade-off framework that goes beyond feature-by-feature comparison into operational fit.
feedback_weaknesses: Some numerical claims are very specific yet weakly supported in-context (e.g., exact global support percentages, cost figures, and latency details presented as facts without inline citation granularity or methodology), which reduces verifiability.

A few technical statements are over-generalized or slightly imprecise, especially around SSE/statelessness and scaling: persistent connections still consume connection/state at the transport/server level, and unicast/broadcast behavior is not universally as simplified as described.
The report relies heavily on marketing/secondary sources from a few vendors and is light on neutral benchmark studies, making it harder to fully validate comparative performance claims.
Potentially outdated or ambiguous temporal claims (e.g., support and version tables) are presented as fixed, without timestamped evidence or variance by platform/version behavior.

Limited discussion of important operational edge cases: intermediary/proxy buffering, HTTP timeout behavior, TLS/session resumption impacts, ordering/replay guarantees under partition/reconnect, and explicit backpressure/flow-control limitations per protocol implementation.
